title: Claude Cowork Setup: Done in 1 Hour
description: Get Claude Cowork fully configured and running your first real task — in one sitting.

modules.title: Get the App Ready
modules.agentInstructions: Everything starts here before you touch anything else.

steps.title: Download Claude Desktop
steps.content: Go to claude.ai, download the desktop app, and install it. Open it and sign in.

steps.title: Upgrade to Pro
steps.content: Go to Settings → Plan → Upgrade to Pro ($20/mo). You need this to access Cowork features. No Pro, no Cowork.

steps.title: Find the Cowork Tab
steps.content: Once you're on Pro, you'll see the **Cowork tab** at the top of the app. Click it. This is where everything lives.

modules.title: Build Your Folder Structure
modules.agentInstructions: Help the learner set up their local folder structure for Claude Cowork.

steps.title: Create Your Main Folder
steps.content: On your computer, create a folder called **CLAUDE COWORK**. This is where all your work will live.

steps.title: Add Your Subfolders
steps.content:
Inside that folder, create these four folders:

- **About Me**
- **Projects**
- **Templates**
- **Output Files**

modules.title: Write Your .md Files
modules.agentInstructions: These two files do the heavy lifting. They replace long prompts forever.

steps.title: Create about-me.md
steps.content:
Inside your **About Me** folder, create a file called `about-me.md`. Write:

- Who you are
- What you do
- How you like to work
- Your tone and communication style

steps.title: Create anti-ai-style.md
steps.content:
Create a second file called `anti-ai-style.md`. Write every word, phrase, and sentence structure you never want Claude to use. Think:

- *"Delve into"*
- *"It's important to note"*
- *"Certainly!"*
- Any buzzwords that sound robotic

modules.title: Set Your Global Instructions
modules.agentInstructions: Help the learner configure their global instructions so Claude reads their context files automatically.

steps.title: Open Global Instructions
steps.content: Go to **Settings → Cowork → Edit Global Instructions.**

steps.title: Write Your Rules
steps.content:
Tell Claude:

- Where your folders are
- Your file naming conventions (e.g. `2024-01-task-name.docx`)
- Which files to read before every task (`about-me.md`, `anti-ai-style.md`)

You write this once. It runs automatically every single time.

modules.title: Create Your First Project
modules.agentInstructions: Help the learner create their first Cowork project with scoped memory.

steps.title: Start a New Project
steps.content: Go to **Cowork → Projects → +**. Name it after a task you do every week.

steps.title: Why This Matters
steps.content: Projects have scoped memory. Claude remembers what it did last session. You stop re-explaining your context every time you open the app.
verificationQuestions: What is the main benefit of using Cowork projects instead of regular chats?
Why does scoped memory reduce the need for long prompts?

modules.title: Run Your First Real Task
modules.agentInstructions: Guide the learner through running their first real task using the prompt structure and scheduling.

steps.title: Use This Prompt Structure
steps.content:
Type: *"I want to [your task]. Ask me questions first."*

Claude will ask you clarifying questions before doing anything. You direct. It builds.

steps.title: Add AskUserQuestion to Every Prompt
steps.content: Always include *"Use AskUserQuestion"* in your prompts. Claude generates a clickable form to fill in — instead of you writing a long prompt every time.

steps.title: Schedule a Repeating Task
steps.content:
Tell Claude: *"Every Monday at 8am, create my weekly work briefing."*

You wake up to a finished document in your Output Files folder. That's the whole point.
